title: Stephan Somogyi discussed how to improve journalist safety
excerpt: Attacks on journalists are evolving and increasing, both in quality and quantity, all over the world. Journalists must keep up and invest effort to protect themselves and the people around them as a core part of their job. Stephan Somogyi, a senior research fellow in Columbia Journalism School, talk about how to think these risks and how to keep yourself and those around you safer.

title: Prof. Adrian Rauchfleisch received 2025 NSTC Outstanding Research Award
excerpt: 2025 NSTC Outstanding Research Award was given to 80 scholars and researchers nationwide, with Professor Adrian Rauchfleisch of the Graduate Institute of Journalism being the only recipient in the field of Communication Studies.

title: Welcome! Assistant Prof. Lokman Tsui has joined the Graduate Institute of Journalism
excerpt: Lokman Tsui grew up in the Netherlands, taught at CityUHK, and worked at Google responsible for freedom of expression in the Asia-Pacific region.

title: Documentary Sharing: Taiwan - The Making of a Nation
excerpt: This work was completed by Neal E. Robbins, who taught at the institute in its early days (1992–1993), through in-depth field interviews and filming. It was created during his visit to the Graduate Institute of Journalism at NTU in 2023 as a visiting scholar. The perspective focuses on Taiwan, presenting his observations and care for this land over the years.

title: Visiting scholar Alexander Haentzschel discussed German media and multinational platforms
excerpt: Alexander Haentzschel, Professor of Media and Communication Studies at SRH University, shared key factors for the success of multinational platforms, the strategies and responses of major German media organizations, the impact of the platform economy on media consumption, and how media companies should respond.
